commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e7308ef13b02da15292040548405a193dcd94f51)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Memory Core doctor state migration and SDK helper to import legacy `state/memory/<agent>.sqlite` rows into each agent's SQLite database and archive the old sidecar.

PR surface: Source +323, Tests +236. Total +559 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main routes memory to per-agent SQLite while the issue shows field evidence of a populated legacy sidecar and empty active store after upgrade.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the code fix must be paired with contributor real upgrade/search proof, which ClawSweeper automation cannot supply for the external PR.

target.title: fix #95495: [Bug]: 2026.6.9 silently relocates memory store with no migration, forcing a full re-embed (1499 files) with zero upgrade-time warning
target.body:
## Summary

- Migrates legacy Memory Core sidecar indexes from `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/memory/<agent>.sqlite` into the per-agent SQLite database through a doctor state migration.
- Keeps normal memory runtime startup from reading old sidecar databases, so clearing or rebuilding the canonical per-agent index does not resurrect stale memory state.
- Adds upgrade regression tests for the empty-canonical startup case, explicit doctor migration import, and already-populated canonical database preservation.

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — this is an update to the existing issue fix PR, not a sibling follow-up to a merged change.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** OpenClaw 2026.6.9 moved the memory index store from `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/memory/<agent>.sqlite` to `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/agents/<agent>/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite`, but the upgrade path only initialized the new per-agent schema and never imported the old sidecar rows.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch gives the legacy sidecar import one explicit persisted-state owner: Memory Core doctor state migration. Runtime startup now continues to open only the canonical per-agent database, while doctor migration performs the one-time sidecar-to-agent import and archives the old sidecar so future startup cannot read stale state.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users upgrading from 2026.6.8-era memory indexes can preserve indexed memory state instead of rebuilding embeddings for large memory trees, while users who deliberately clear or rebuild the canonical index are protected from old sidecar rows reappearing during ordinary startup.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Memory Core doctor state migrations own the migration contract for legacy persisted state; `ensureMemoryIndexSchema()` owns only the canonical schema for the database it is given.
- **What did NOT change:** This PR only touches Memory Core memory-index migration and the host SDK helper needed by that migration. It does not change provider routing, embedding generation, memory search ranking, session transcript storage, or normal runtime database selection.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause persisted-state migration ownership fix.
- **Related PR scan:** `check-upstream-fixed` reported `CONTINUE_ALLOWED`; `origin/main` did not contain an equivalent fix for PR #95631 at the checked head.

## What Changed

- Removed the legacy sidecar path from runtime schema startup in `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts:790`.
- Exposed `importLegacyMemorySidecarIndex()` as an explicit schema helper in `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts:320` and re-exported it through the host SDK subpaths.
- Added doctor state migration `memory-core-legacy-sidecar-index-to-agent-sqlite` in `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.ts:435`.
- Archives migrated sidecar files as `.migrated` after explicit doctor migration handling, including the already-populated canonical database case.
- Added regression tests in `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts:93` and `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts:400`.
- Updated the plugin SDK surface budget/test to account for the one new public callable SDK helper exported for doctor migration.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** This update supplies current-head code changes, verification, and PR body disclosure so the author-waiting label can be re-evaluated.
- **RF-002:** Added tests for both stale sidecar cases: empty canonical startup leaves the sidecar untouched, and already-populated canonical state remains authoritative during doctor migration.
- **RF-003:** Runtime startup no longer imports an old sidecar when the canonical per-agent index is empty.
- **RF-004:** The persisted-state choice now belongs to doctor state migration instead of startup, and this PR documents the local proof boundary below.
- **RF-005:** The legacy sidecar import was moved out of `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts` runtime startup.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts` and `extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** A legacy sidecar exists beside an empty canonical per-agent database; schema startup must leave canonical rows empty. A doctor migration run against a valid legacy sidecar must import rows into the per-agent database and archive the sidecar. A doctor migration run when canonical rows already exist must preserve canonical rows and archive the old sidecar.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests use real `node:sqlite` databases and real schema/migration code paths, so they catch both the original missing-import upgrade path and the ClawSweeper stale-sidecar startup path without mocking the storage layer.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.state-migration.test.ts
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/engine-storage.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-storage.ts && git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:core:test
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Upgrading from the legacy memory sidecar store can preserve existing memory index rows through an explicit doctor state migration, while normal runtime startup no longer resurrects stale sidecar state when the canonical per-agent database is empty.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree at current head, using real `node:sqlite` databases in temporary state directories through the Memory Core schema, doctor migration, and manager test paths.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-95631
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.state-migration.test.ts
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.ts extensions/memory-core/doctor-contract-api.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/engine-storage.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-storage.ts && git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:core:test
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 183.01s
Unit shard: 1 test file passed, 7 tests passed
Commands shard: 1 test file passed, 6 tests passed
Extension-memory shard: 3 test files passed, 32 tests passed

Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.

pnpm tsgo:core exited 0
pnpm tsgo:core:test exited 0
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The doctor migration imports legacy sidecar rows into the per-agent SQLite database and archives the sidecar; if the per-agent index already has rows, doctor migration preserves canonical rows and archives the stale sidecar; normal schema startup leaves an empty canonical database empty even when an old sidecar exists.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a packaged release-beta upgrade install or any external embedding provider. The proof here is local source-level upgrade-state proof with real SQLite files, not third-party live proof.
- **Unavailable environment/context:** No external provider account, STS path, packaged beta install, or remote customer state directory was used in this verification; the completed verification is local upgrade-state proof against real SQLite state files.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** auth-provider, automation, compatibility, security-boundary, session-state.
- **Risk explanation:** The auth-provider and security-boundary signals come from copying existing embedding cache columns, not from changing provider authentication or request signing. The compatibility and session-state risks are the intended migration surface: legacy memory index state moves into the canonical per-agent SQLite database. The automation risk is limited to adding a doctor state migration entry.
- **Why acceptable:** The import only runs through the explicit doctor migration path, validates the legacy sidecar schema shape before copying rows, skips overwrite when canonical index rows already exist, and archives handled sidecar files to prevent later stale startup reads. Normal runtime startup no longer opens the legacy sidecar.
- **Patch quality notes:** Added broad catches are bounded to migration discovery/per-source reporting so one unreadable legacy source does not abort the whole doctor migration; the schema import catch rolls back a savepoint and rethrows. The `return null` path is the existing doctor contract for no detected legacy state. The larger diff is mostly SQLite fixture and regression coverage needed to prove the migration ownership boundary.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** 88/100; the remaining evidence boundary is that this was verified locally with real SQLite state files rather than a packaged release-beta upgrade install or external provider run.
target.author: mushuiyu886
